title: Galleries and Creative Studios Help
description: Portfolio, inquiry, proposal, local visibility, and lightweight workflow systems for galleries, studios, and creative firms.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC gallery or studio that won’t have a marketing department.

Portfolio, inquiry, proposals, visit requests, event interest, follow-up — sized for the 1–2 person studio, not the global gallery network.

The kind of inquiry-to-sale system a Gagosian or Artsy runs — sized for a one-room gallery that needs to track visitors without a CRM team.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • A public site that explains the work without flattening the taste.
  • Inquiry paths for commissions, visits, events, or retainers.
  • Proposal and follow-up systems that reduce manual copying.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Webflow, Squarespace, Airtable, Notion, Google Workspace, email, analytics.

      03

      Common misfit

      The work looks strong in person but the online path does not qualify or organize demand.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for galleries and creative studios.

      For galleries and creative studios, the public site needs taste, but the operating setup needs discipline. Portfolio pages, inquiry types, visit requests, proposals, event interest, and collector or client follow-up need to land somewhere useful.

      The first fix is usually a clearer intake path: what the visitor wants, what proof they need, who should respond, and what happens if the lead is not ready today. That can be light, but it cannot be invisible.

      • Compare Webflow and Squarespace for Manhattan businesses
      • Build clearer portfolio and inquiry pages
      • Create a lightweight inquiry system

          Map inquiry types, proof, response templates, proposal steps, and owner visibility.

title: Medical and Wellness Help
description: Trust, booking, intake, local visibility, and workflow systems for small medical and wellness practices that need cleaner digital operations.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC practice fighting Mount Sinai and CityMD for patients.

Trust, local visibility, booking, intake, internal workflow — sized for the 2-doctor practice, not the hospital system.

The patient-trust and intake setup a hospital outpost has — sized for the corner medical practice that doesn’t have a marketing department or an IT director.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • Trust-forward service pages and clear patient/client paths.
  • Booking and intake that reduce phone tag.
  • Careful handoffs, privacy-aware workflow, and local visibility.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Practice booking tools, Google profile, forms, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365, analytics.

      03

      Common misfit

      Scheduling, forms, staff handoffs, local search, and service information are patched together.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for medical and wellness practices.

      For small medical and wellness practices, the site has to build trust while the workflow handles appointment interest, intake, reminders, staff handoffs, and follow-up with care. The system should feel calm for patients and manageable for the team.

      The first move is usually to separate public clarity from private workflow. We check what the site says, what the form collects, where the request goes, and what staff need to do next without exposing sensitive details in the wrong place.

      • Strengthen Google visibility and Google profile basics
      • Clarify trust and service pages
      • Clean up intake and staff handoff

title: Professional Services Help
description: Service pages, lead qualification, customer list, proposals, reporting, and local visibility systems for small professional firms that need cleaner handoffs.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC professional firm fighting the big-name consultancies for the same clients.

Service pages, lead qualification, customer list, proposals, reporting, local visibility — sized for the 5-person practice, not the 50-partner firm.

The same kind of intake, proposal, and reporting setup a Big 4 firm runs — sized for an independent NYC practice that doesn’t have a back office department.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • Service pages that clarify expertise and fit.
  • Lead intake that qualifies the right prospects.
  • customer list or owner view visibility so follow-up does not depend on memory.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Webflow, Squarespace, HubSpot, Airtable, Notion, Google Workspace, Calendly, analytics.

      03

      Common misfit

      Expertise is buried, leads are not qualified, and proposals live across email, docs, and memory.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for professional service firms.

      For professional service firms, the digital setup has to earn trust, qualify the right prospects, capture the context, and keep proposals, follow-up, and reporting from scattering across inboxes and memory.

      The first fix is often lead qualification. We look at service pages, forms, customer list or spreadsheet use, proposal steps, and owner visibility so the team can decide faster without adding enterprise software weight.

      • Clarify service pages and proof
      • Build a lead and proposal workflow

title: Restaurants and Bars Help
description: Website, POS, reservations, reviews, local search, and owner reporting systems for New York restaurants and bars running on tight margins.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC restaurant fighting for tables when Sweetgreen opens down the block.

Website, POS, reservations, online ordering, reviews, owner reporting — sized for the 30-seat spot, not the 50-location chain.

Toast costs your local cafe what it costs a chain restaurant. We help NYC food & bev get the same caliber of POS + booking + ordering setup at a fraction of the bill.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • Menu and service pages that load fast on mobile.
  • Reservation, ordering, phone, and map paths that are obvious.
  • Local search pages tied to neighborhood demand.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Toast, Square, Resy, Tock, Google profile, Instagram, email, analytics.

      03

      Common misfit

      Menus go stale, POS links sit apart from the website, reviews are unmanaged, and private-event leads disappear into inboxes.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for restaurants and bars.

      For restaurants and bars, the website is only one part of the operating chain. The menu, Google profile, reservation tool, POS link, private-event form, review path, and owner reporting all affect whether a customer turns into revenue or another loose thread.

      The first move is usually to trace one real customer path: search, menu, booking or order, payment, reminder, review, and follow-up. That shows which tools are worth keeping and which handoffs need to be connected or rebuilt.

      • Compare Square and Toast for Manhattan restaurants
      • Build the Google visibility layer
      • Plan the system behind the work

title: Retail and E-commerce Help
description: Inventory, Shopify or Square, local pickup, product pages, email capture, and local search systems for NYC retail teams that need cleaner operations.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC shop competing with Amazon Prime for the same customer.

Website, inventory, local pickup, product pages, email capture, local search — sized to actually compete with Amazon’s convenience without Amazon’s budget.

Amazon spends millions on the customer experience. We give you 1% of that budget’s caliber, sized for the storefront on your block.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • Product and category pages that support local discovery.
  • Inventory, pickup, shipping, POS, and email connected cleanly.
  • Reporting that shows what is selling and where demand comes from.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Shopify, Square, Squarespace, Google Merchant, email platforms, POS, analytics.

      03

      Common misfit

      Online products, in-store inventory, local pickup, and email lists do not match.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for retail and e-commerce businesses.

      For retail and e-commerce teams, the system has to reconcile what people see online with what staff know in the shop. Product data, pickup options, POS inventory, email capture, return questions, and reporting all need a practical owner view.

      A stronger platform is not automatically the answer. Sometimes the right move is cleaner product structure, better collection pages, simpler pickup logic, or a owner view that makes sales and demand signals visible.

      • Compare Shopify and Squarespace for NYC retail
      • Improve product and collection pages
      • Estimate software drag

title: Salons and Wellness Help
description: Booking, staff calendars, service pages, reviews, rebooking, and local search systems for salons and wellness teams that need cleaner flow.
html:
Industry help

Tech for the NYC salon fighting Sephora for product sales and Drybar for clients.

Booking, staff calendars, deposits, reminders, reviews, rebooking — sized for the chair count you have, not the franchise count Mindbody is pricing for.

The booking-and-payment setup a chain salon has — sized for a 3-chair shop, not a 50-location franchise.

Published May 4, 2026

Page sections

01

Website needs

  • Clear services, pricing context, and booking paths.
  • Staff availability and repeat-client flow.
  • Review and local search support for neighborhood discovery.
      • 02

      Common tools

      Square Appointments, GlossGenius, Fresha, Mindbody, Momence, Google profile, email.

      03

      Common misfit

      Booking works, but service pages, staff schedules, reviews, reminders, and follow-up do not reinforce each other.

      Fit map

      What usually has to line up for salons and wellness teams.

      For salons and wellness teams, the setup has to support discovery, services, staff calendars, deposits, reminders, reviews, and rebooking without making the owner reconcile everything by hand after a busy day.

      The best first fix is often the booking path, but the booking path depends on service pages, Google visibility, review cues, intake questions, and follow-up. We map those pieces together before recommending another platform.

      • Compare Square Appointments and GlossGenius
      • Clarify service pages and booking paths
      • Clean up intake and follow-up
